plan.annotation: Left-to-right flow: Client → Portable Protocol (initialize, stage, commit, restore) → Validation Engine (validation before commit) → Adapter Boundary → Memory, Convex, and Postgres adapters. The adapter boundary is the one marked trust boundary; everything right of it is backend-specific.

operations.label: Editable nodes
operations.value: Client, Portable Protocol, Validation Engine, Adapter Boundary, Memory Adapter, Convex Adapter, Postgres Adapter — each a native editable shape

operations.label: Typed connectors
operations.value: Labeled connectors: initialize, stage, commit, restore from Client through Protocol; 'validation before commit' gate into the adapter boundary; persistence links to each adapter

operations.label: Trust boundary
operations.value: Adapter Boundary rendered as one visibly distinct divider separating portable protocol from backend-specific adapters

plan.pptxFallback: If native rendering is unavailable, the slide exports as grouped editable PowerPoint shapes and labeled connectors — never paragraph arrows — preserving node and connector editability per the fallback policy.
plan.takeaway: A single portable protocol carries client operations through validation to backend-specific adapters behind one trust boundary.
plan.title: NodeSlide Portable Protocol Architecture
